steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most effectively uses information from the notes to specify the test site's climate. The notes state the site has "a subtropic climate with winter rain."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A test was conducted in Israel to study the effect of plant litter on seedling emergence in a given climate.: Discusses the test's purpose “in a given climate” but never names or describes the actual climate — no specification. ✗
  • Choice B — Boeken and Orenstein tested the effect of dead leaves and other plant material on seedling emergence in Israel.: Describes plant litter and the location (Israel) but says nothing about the climate type. ✗
  • Choice C — Boeken and Orenstein's study was conducted in a subtropic climate with winter rain.: Directly quotes the climate description from the notes, word for word — fully specifies the climate. ✓
  • Choice D — In a 2001 study, Boeken and Orenstein found that the presence of plant litter had a positive effect on seedling emergence.: States a finding about plant litter's positive effect but includes no climate information at all. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
